channel.title: NPR Topics: Economy
channel.description: NPR news on the U.S. and world economy, the World Bank, and Federal Reserve. Commentary on economic trends. Subscribe to NPR Economy podcasts and RSS feeds.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Door shuts on some immigrant entrepreneurs as U.S. restricts small-business loans
item.description: For decades, immigrants who are legal permanent residents in the U.S. could get loans through the Small Business Administration, a core pillar of small-business lending. Not anymore.
item.content:encoded:
For decades, immigrants who are legal permanent residents in the U.S. could get loans through the Small Business Administration, a core pillar of small-business lending. Not anymore.

(Image credit: Courtesy of Sayuri Tsuchitani)
item.dc:creator: Alina Selyukh

item.title: Can smartphones help explain the drop in birth rates?
item.description: Are smartphones causing people to have fewer children? A provocative new working paper explores the persistent drop in birth rates since the iPhone was introduced nearly two decades ago.
item.content:encoded:
Are smartphones causing people to have fewer children? A provocative new working paper explores the persistent drop in birth rates since the iPhone was introduced nearly two decades ago.

(Image credit: David Paul Morris)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Fueled by soaring gas prices, inflation tops 4% for the first time in 3 years
item.description: Inflation has jumped to its highest level in more than three years. Much of the increase is fueled by higher gasoline prices since the U.S. launched its war with Iran.
item.content:encoded: Inflation has jumped to its highest level in more than three years. Much of the increase is fueled by higher gasoline prices since the U.S. launched its war with Iran.
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Inflation has hit a 3-year high. What it means and what the Fed might do
item.description: The U.S. annual inflation rate jumped to 4.2% in May, a three-year high. NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with economist Claudia Sahm about what the numbers mean and what the Fed may do about it.
item.content:encoded: The U.S. annual inflation rate jumped to 4.2% in May, a three-year high. NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with economist Claudia Sahm about what the numbers mean and what the Fed may do about it.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Inflation tops 4% for the first time in 3 years on spike in gasoline prices
item.description: Inflation has surged to its highest level in more than three years since the U.S. and Israel launched their war on Iran, triggering a surge in gasoline prices.
item.content:encoded:
Inflation has surged to its highest level in more than three years since the U.S. and Israel launched their war on Iran, triggering a surge in gasoline prices.

(Image credit: Joe Raedle)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: Nearly half of U.S. families couldn't afford basic necessities in 2024, report finds
item.description: A new report finds close to half of American families did not earn enough to cover necessities in 2024. And with prices continuing to rise, there's little sign that's changed.
item.content:encoded: A new report finds close to half of American families did not earn enough to cover necessities in 2024. And with prices continuing to rise, there's little sign that's changed.
item.dc:creator: Stephan Bisaha

item.title: From side gigs to canceling trips, Americans adjust to months of high gas prices
item.description: Months of higher gas prices are taking a toll. We check in on the trade-offs people are making.
item.content:encoded: Months of higher gas prices are taking a toll. We check in on the trade-offs people are making.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Ludden

item.title: Social Security funds could run short by 2032, program's Trustees warn
item.description: A trust fund that helps to finance Social Security benefits is expected to run out of money in less than seven years — unless Congress acts to patch the system before that.
item.content:encoded:
A trust fund that helps to finance Social Security benefits is expected to run out of money in less than seven years — unless Congress acts to patch the system before that.

(Image credit: Kevin Dietsch)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: From side gigs to canceling trips, Americans adjust to months of high gas prices
item.description: Months of higher gas prices are taking a toll. We check in on the trade-offs people are making.
item.content:encoded: Months of higher gas prices are taking a toll. We check in on the trade-offs people are making.
item.dc:creator: Jennifer Ludden

item.title: The U.S. adds 172,000 jobs. Many are in restaurants, bars and hotels
item.description: U.S. employers added jobs for the third month in a row in May, while the unemployment rate held steady at 4.3%. But wage gains softened and likely failed to keep pace with rising prices.
item.content:encoded:
U.S. employers added jobs for the third month in a row in May, while the unemployment rate held steady at 4.3%. But wage gains softened and likely failed to keep pace with rising prices.

(Image credit: Brandon Bell)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley
